stem: A 3-qubit circuit applies X to qubit 0 only, then measures qubit i into classical bit i. In the Sampler's counts, which bitstring dominates?
code:
qc = QuantumCircuit(3, 3)
qc.x(0)                       # only qubit 0 is flipped
qc.measure(0, 0)
qc.measure(1, 1)
qc.measure(2, 2)
# ... transpile, then sampler.run([isa]) ...
counts = result[0].data.c.get_counts()

options.text: `'001'` — Qiskit prints bitstrings little-endian, so classical bit 0 (qubit 0) is the rightmost character
`'100'` — qubit 0 is the leftmost character in the bitstring
`'111'` — flipping any qubit flips the whole register
`'000'` — X on qubit 0 has no effect on the measured bits

explanation.correct: Qiskit uses little-endian bit ordering: in the printed bitstring the classical bit with the highest index is on the left and bit 0 is on the right. Only qubit 0 is excited, and it is measured into classical bit 0, so the rightmost character is 1 and the rest are 0 -> '001'.

distractors.B: This assumes big-endian ordering (qubit 0 leftmost). Qiskit is little-endian, so qubit 0 lands in the rightmost position, giving '001', not '100'.
distractors.C: X acts only on the qubit it targets; qubits 1 and 2 stay in |0>. The register does not collectively flip.
distractors.D: X on qubit 0 flips it to |1>, so its measured bit is 1, not 0 — the outcome cannot be all zeros.

verification.proof_script:
from qiskit import QuantumCircuit
from qiskit.transpiler.preset_passmanagers import generate_preset_pass_manager
from qiskit_ibm_runtime import SamplerV2
from qiskit_ibm_runtime.fake_provider import FakeManilaV2

backend = FakeManilaV2()
pm = generate_preset_pass_manager(backend=backend, optimization_level=1)
qc = QuantumCircuit(3, 3)
qc.x(0)                      # flip qubit 0 only
qc.measure(0, 0); qc.measure(1, 1); qc.measure(2, 2)
isa = pm.run(qc)

sampler = SamplerV2(mode=backend)
sampler.options.simulator.seed_simulator = 123
counts = sampler.run([isa], shots=2000).result()[0].data.c.get_counts()
top = max(counts, key=counts.get)

candidates = {"A": "001", "B": "100", "C": "111", "D": "000"}
evidence, proven = {}, []
for k, v in candidates.items():
    match = (v == top)
    evidence[k] = f"option claims dominant bitstring {v!r}; observed argmax={top!r} (counts[{v!r}]={counts.get(v,0)}) -> match={match}"
    if match:
        proven.append(k)

emit_verdict({"answer": proven, "evidence": evidence,
              "observed": {"counts": counts, "dominant": top}})
